title: pimpinella benthamii
intro: Pimpinella benthamii, also known as Bunium or Indian anise, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family that is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Pimpinella benthamii include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Pimpinella benthamii include the use of its seeds, roots, and leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
